input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-22T02:40:35.978Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which was removed in MSTest v4. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and then provide the fix.

data.content: MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()`, which inlines the assertion into the test body.
data.reasoningText:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which has been around since v2). Let me update the test file.

In MSTest v4, the recommended replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<TException>(() => ...)` which was introduced in MSTest v3.3+ and is the preferred pattern in v4.

Let me update both the .csproj to use MSTest v4 and the test file to use `Assert.ThrowsExactly`.

toolCalls.name: edit
